Text: We do not need a constitutional amendment for children to pray, and what I did was ask the Department of Justice and the Department of Education to issue, for the first time, a set of guidelines that we gave to every school in America, stating that children could not be prevented from engaging in religious activity when they prayed, when they did everything they could do according to the constitution, simply because they were on public school grounds, and I think anyone who has experienced it will tell you that our administration has done more than any other in years to clarify religious freedom in public spaces, including in public schools.
source: We don't need a constitutional amendment for kids to pray, and what I did was to have the justice department and the education department for the first time ever issue a set of guidelines that we gave to every school in America, saying that children could not be interfered with in religious advocacy when they were praying, when they were doing whatever they could do under the constitution, just because they were on a public school ground, and I think anyone who's experienced this will tell you that our administration has done more than any in years to clarify the freedom of religion in the public square, including in the public schools.

Text: We do not need a constitutional amendment for children to pray, and what I did was have the Department of Justice and the Department of Education, for the first time, issue a set of guidelines that we delivered to every school in the United States, stating that children could not be interfered with in their religious observance when they were praying, when they were doing what they could do under the Constitution, just because they were on public school grounds, and I believe anyone who has experienced this will tell you that our administration has done more than any in years to clarify the freedom of religion in the public square, even in public schools.

Text: And if we can do that and do it in a way that allows for deeper cuts on the Soviet side where they should come from, then I think we have the opportunity in the long term to shift some of our resources from the military to important domestic priorities that can provide study opportunities for that young woman whose mother just wrote to me from Texas, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father, who have a child studying in the first year at the university, an electrical engineering student, a very smart student, and they cannot afford to keep this child in university.
source: And if we can do that and do it in a way that gets deeper cuts on the Soviet side, which is where they ought to come from, then I think we have an opportunity over the long haul to begin to move some of our resources from the military to important domestic priorities that can provide college opportunity for that young woman whose mother wrote me from Texas just the other day, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a child that's a freshman in college, an electrical engineering major, a very bright student and they can't afford to keep that child in college.

Text: And if we can do that and do it in a way that we achieve deeper cuts on the Soviet side, which is where they should come from, then I believe we have the long-term opportunity to start moving some of our resources from the military sphere to important domestic priorities that can provide university opportunities for that young woman whose mother wrote to me from Texas just a few days ago, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a son who is a freshman in college, majoring in electrical engineering, a very bright student, and they cannot afford to keep that son in college.
source: And if we can do that and do it in a way that gets deeper cuts on the Soviet side, which is where they ought to come from, then I think we have an opportunity over the long haul to begin to move some of our resources from the military to important domestic priorities that can provide college opportunity for that young woman whose mother wrote me from Texas just the other day, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a child that's a freshman in college, an electrical engineering major, a very bright student and they can't afford to keep that child in college.

Text: And if we can do that and do it in a way that achieves greater reductions on the Soviet side, where they should come from, then I think we have the long-term opportunity to start shifting some of our resources from the military to important domestic priorities that can offer educational opportunities to that young woman whose mother wrote to me from Texas the other day, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a child who is a freshman in college, studying electrical engineering, a very bright student, and they cannot afford to keep this child in college.
source: And if we can do that and do it in a way that gets deeper cuts on the Soviet side, which is where they ought to come from, then I think we have an opportunity over the long haul to begin to move some of our resources from the military to important domestic priorities that can provide college opportunity for that young woman whose mother wrote me from Texas just the other day, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a child that's a freshman in college, an electrical engineering major, a very bright student and they can't afford to keep that child in college.

Text: And if we can do that and do it in a way that results in deeper cuts on the Soviet side, wherever they may have to come from, then I think we have the opportunity in the long term to shift some of our resources from the military to important domestic priorities that can provide college opportunities to that young woman whose mother just wrote me from Texas, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a child who is a freshman in college, an electrical engineering major, a very smart student and they cannot afford to keep that child in college.
source: And if we can do that and do it in a way that gets deeper cuts on the Soviet side, which is where they ought to come from, then I think we have an opportunity over the long haul to begin to move some of our resources from the military to important domestic priorities that can provide college opportunity for that young woman whose mother wrote me from Texas just the other day, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a child that's a freshman in college, an electrical engineering major, a very bright student and they can't afford to keep that child in college.

Text: And if we can do it and do it in a way that achieves greater reductions on the Soviet side, where they should come from, then I think we have the opportunity, in the long term, to begin to transfer some of our resources from the military sector to important domestic priorities that can offer a university opportunity to this young woman whose mother wrote to me from Texas just a few days ago, from Longview, Texas: two teachers, a mother and a father who have a freshman studying electrical engineering at the university, a very bright student, and they cannot afford to keep this child in college.
